content.full_text:
Oct 20 [illegible]


Oct. 29, 1991

Ms Alice Hughes
Calif. Fair Political
Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 958

Dear Ms Hughes:

As a rank amateur in this field of Politics, and having goofed
once, Mr Baugher told me to contact you for advice assistance.
Would you please answer for me these three questions?

(1)Is this correct for candidates to use the same mailing permit?
last year there were several mailings using Mr Brents permit, but
he was not a candidate. I am not a candidate against either of
these two individuals, however I am running. It seems to me this
practice has the potential of obscuring the true amount spent by
candidates.

(2) Not many here were aware of Form 502, I wonder if all of
these candidates sent in this form?

(3) Last if we spend less than $200.00 is it required to be
reported? If so what form is needed, and what time frame after
the election? Thanking you in advance.

Sincerely,

Charles E. Alsop

sections.question_synthetic: The sender asks three questions: whether candidates can share mailing permits, whether all candidates are required to file Form 502, and what reporting requirements apply to campaign spending under $200.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. Letter appears to be from a candidate seeking guidance on three separate compliance questions. Minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'Eorm' for 'Form', 'sPent' for 'spent').
